summary: Blocking calcium channels prevents Ca²⁺ influx which inhibits ACh release; therefore, nothing among the listed options increases it significantly under normal conditions. The primary mechanism involves calcium entering through voltage-gated channels leading to neurotransmitter release. Thus, none of the other channel blockers will enhance ACh release as they do not open pathways allowing more Ca²⁺ inside the presynaptic terminals.
